description_long: This mosque features a grand central structure with a large, ribbed dome flanked by two smaller domes, all topped with pointed finials. The main building is surrounded by a series of arched colonnades and semi-circular porticos, giving it an open and inviting appearance. Two tall, slender minarets with conical roofs rise prominently from the main structure, while a separate, cylindrical minaret with spiral stairs stands nearby. The surfaces are predominantly a pale, marble-like stone with subtle greenish-blue patina on the domes and minaret roofs, suggesting weathered copper or oxidized metal. The windows and arches are adorned with delicate tracery, but no visible geometric patterns or calligraphy are present. The overall design blends elements of Ottoman and fantasy architecture, with an emphasis on verticality and ornate rooflines.
description: A grand mosque with a large central dome, two tall minarets, and arched colonnades, featuring pale stone and greenish-blue domed roofs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, large central dome, two flanking minarets, and a separate spiral minaret to the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the layered domes, arched porticos, and the height of the main minarets behind the central structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tall minarets, the back of the main dome, and the separate spiral minaret on the right.
description_view_3: Angled view focusing on the spiral minaret's staircase and the arrangement of domes and porticos around the main building.
attribute_dome: Three domes are visible: one large central dome and two smaller domes, all ribbed and topped with pointed finials, with a greenish-blue patina suggesting oxidized copper.
attribute_minaret: Three minarets are visible: two tall, slender minarets attached to the main structure and one separate cylindrical minaret with an external spiral staircase, all with conical roofs and decorative finials.
attribute_architectural_style: Ottoman-inspired with fantasy elements, characterized by large domes, tall slender minarets, arched colonnades, and ornate rooflines.
